name: Lark / Feishu
description: Independent third-party Lark/Feishu AgentApp for the official lark-cli. It teaches agents to discover and use the CLI's own commands, schemas, embedded skills, auth flow, and risk signals instead of reimplementing Lark APIs.

references.title: Official lark-cli source and discovery contract
references.description: What the AgentApp relies on from the official Lark/Feishu CLI and what it intentionally does not duplicate.

references.title: CLI-assisted user consent flow
references.description: Portable auth guidance based on lark-cli config/auth commands and device-flow continuation.

references.title: Live validation discipline
references.description: Real-device checks for installed CLI discovery, auth status, read actions, write previews, and risk handling.

interfaces.title: Lark and Feishu CLI
interfaces.description: Official CLI interface for discovering and operating Lark or Feishu messaging, documents, calendars, tasks, approvals, and other services.
interfaces.whenToUse: Use for Lark or Feishu work after discovering the relevant official CLI guidance and inspecting the CLI's current service schema.

discovery.installHint: If `lark-cli` is missing, inspect the official install guidance and install it only when the current user task calls for making the capability usable.
discovery.discoveryHint: Run `lark-cli --help`, `lark-cli doctor`, `lark-cli whoami`, `lark-cli skills list`, `lark-cli skills read <skill>`, service-specific help, and `lark-cli schema <service.resource.method>` as needed.

requirements.authDiscovery: Use the CLI-reported authorization URL and continuation instructions. Prefer the CLI's non-blocking device-code flow when available; for a localhost callback, complete consent in a browser that can reach the listener.

effects.notes: Actual effects depend on the selected Lark service method; inspect its schema and risk metadata before execution.
